title: Phlix plugin manifest
description: Schema for plugin.json files consumed by the Phlix plugin loader. See docs/plugins/manifest.md for the human-readable spec. Unknown top-level fields are permitted here because the parser (Phlix\Shared\Plugin\Manifest) reports them separately as soft `unknown_field` warnings rather than hard schema errors.

name.description: Globally unique kebab-case identifier; must start with 'phlix-plugin-'. Segments are joined by single hyphens (no leading, trailing, or doubled hyphens).

version.description: Semver version of the plugin itself.

phlix_min_server_version.description: Minimum Phlix server semver the plugin is compatible with.

type.description: Plugin category. Mirrors the cases of Phlix\Shared\Plugin\ManifestType.

entry.description: Fully-qualified class name of the plugin entry point.

events.description: Manifest aliases (e.g., 'phlix.playback.started', 'phlix.user.logged_in') the plugin subscribes to. The loader (A.4) resolves aliases to event class FQCNs. Segments after the leading 'phlix.<area>' may contain underscores (e.g. 'logged_in').

settings.description: User-configurable settings exposed by the plugin. Keyed by setting name.

type.description: Value type. 'integer'/'boolean' are accepted aliases of 'int'/'bool'.

label.description: Human-readable field label shown in the plugin settings UI.

description.description: Help text shown beneath the field in the plugin settings UI.

link.description: Optional URL where the operator obtains this value (e.g. an API-key signup or docs page), rendered as a help link beside the field in the settings UI.

link_text.description: Optional anchor text for `link`; the settings UI falls back to a generic label when absent.

signature.description: Optional sha256 signature of the plugin payload, or null when unsigned. Verification logic lives in A.4.
